.mvn/wrapper/MavenWrapperDownloader.java.general_idea: Update Apache License URL from HTTP to HTTPS for security best practices.

.mvn/wrapper/MavenWrapperDownloader.java.after:
private static final String DEFAULT_DOWNLOAD_URL =
        "https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/io/takari/maven-wrapper/" + WRAPPER_VERSION + "/maven-wrapper-" + WRAPPER_VERSION + ".jar";

.mvn/wrapper/MavenWrapperDownloader.java.general_idea: Reformat constant assignments to single lines for MAVEN_WRAPPER_PROPERTIES_PATH and MAVEN_WRAPPER_JAR_PATH.

Reformat nested if-statements and print statements for directory creation to improve indentation and line breaks.
Reformat anonymous Authenticator class instantiation to align braces and parameters.

Add import for CRLFLogConverter to support safe logging markers.

Remove the ConstraintViolationProblemModule bean and its import as it is no longer required or compatible with the current dependency set.

Reorder method parameters to place the custom @LiquibaseDataSource provider after LiquibaseProperties, likely to align with Spring Boot 3.x / JHipster 8.x bean definition expectations or to resolve ambiguity in dependency injection.
Remove unused imports related to deprecated Spring Security classes.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: While the old syntax compiles on Java 17, the intent is to modernize code to use Java 16+ features; however, strictly speaking, the old code still compiles. But in the context of a 'migration to next Java version' where modernization is key, this is often treated as a breaking change if the target is strictly enforcing modern syntax or if the old code was part of a larger refactor. Wait, rule 3 says 'Default to polishment when in doubt'. The old code compiles on J17. So this is polishment.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The exception is never thrown for standard charsets in modern Java, so the try-catch is dead code. The old code compiles, so this is polishment.
